reflection:
Pain at 2. Three days ago it was 6. The last time your anxiety was anything above zero was April 16th, the rebound day. You've been off amitriptyline since the 13th and this is where you landed: better, not worse. That's a meaningful data point.

Your first thought yesterday morning was a dream about traveling with cosita. No body checking, no work alarm, just a fun image that made you smile. That's your nervous system at rest, not on guard.

The pillow fix at 3am is worth noticing too. You woke with neck pain, you found a better combination in the dark, and you went back to sleep. You didn't spiral, you solved it. That's not the same brain that was waking at 5am in a cortisol flood a few weeks ago.

I don't have your evening notes from the Renzo session, so I can't tell you what came up in that room. But you showed up, after weeks of preparation, and that counts.

Beata is tomorrow, Wednesday 23 April at 09:30. Before you get there, write down what Renzo found and what you felt, even 3-4 lines. Body memory fades fast and she'll want to know what the osteopath touched.
opener: Pain at 2, anxiety at zero. The London week is doing something real.
focus_for_today: Write 3-4 lines about the Renzo session before the detail fades. Beata is tomorrow.
